page: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled public IPv4 addresses, though it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32ls_v6 demonstrates strong performance in compute-heavy and memory-bandwidth-sensitive tasks. It achieves a strong Geekbench multi-core score of 16898.0 and a single-core score of 2042.0. Large-block read-only memory bandwidth is strong at 205.24 GB/sec, while write bandwidth averages 87.97 GB/sec. The processor's vector and matrix extensions contribute to strong performance in image processing and text processing, alongside top-tier text generation speeds of 347.06 tokens/sec for small LLM models. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput also rank in the strong tier.

The qualitative cost profile of the D32ls_v6 is defined by its high compute density relative to its memory footprint. By limiting memory to 2.0 GB per core and omitting local storage, this server type offers a cost-efficient option for workloads that require high-frequency CPU cycles but do not demand massive RAM allocations. Tradeoffs include the lack of local NVMe storage and a lower memory ceiling, which makes it less suitable for memory-intensive databases. It is best suited for compute-bound workloads such as web serving, software compilation, image processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv6 family, powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 16 physical cores, a 260 MB L3 cache, and 64.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB. The instance lacks local storage but is Premium Storage capable. In benchmarks, the D32ls_v6 delivers strong performance in Geekbench multi-core testing, large-block memory reads, and static web serving. It also achieves top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models. This server type is a cost-efficient choice for high-compute workloads that do not require extensive memory or local disk space, making it highly suitable for web serving, software compilation, image processing, and lightweight inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong performance in web serving and text processing.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v6 is a compute-optimized VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and strong performance in web serving and text processing.
tagline: A compute-dense, low-memory virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors optimized for web serving and lightweight inference workloads.

Configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core
Strong benchmark performance in static web serving, image processing, and text processing
Top-tier text generation speeds for small language model inference
Lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage configurations
